title: History Editing, Rebase, and Selective Transfer · Repository Trace: Interactive Rebase
summary: Prepare a reviewable local series while preserving authorship intent, conflict evidence, and a recovery reference. Planned artifact: Add and verify the git interactive rebase increment in a history-rewrite plan and verification graph. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use pick, reword, edit, squash, fixup, drop, exec, and reorder with dependency and test awareness.
Explain rebase as selecting commits, resetting a base, and replaying new commits with new identities.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-basics cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Predict Interactive Rebase
steps.instruction:
theory case — Local series has commits parse → test → docs atop old main; new main changes the parser contract. Plan replay, conflict tests, range-diff, recovery ref, and force-with-lease boundary.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Interactive Rebase contract for the supplied data.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for use pick, reword, edit, squash, fixup, drop, exec, and reorder with dependency and test awareness.

steps.title: Bound Interactive Rebase
steps.instruction:
theory case — Local series has commits parse → test → docs atop old main; new main changes the parser contract. Plan replay, conflict tests, range-diff, recovery ref, and force-with-lease boundary.

After tracing the worked code, choose the Interactive Rebase application whose preconditions and limits still match the supplied case.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for use pick, reword, edit, squash, fixup, drop, exec, and reorder with dependency and test awareness.

content.text: Use pick, reword, edit, squash, fixup, drop, exec, and reorder with dependency and test awareness. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Locate repository and operation state, compare working tree, index, refs, and graph, preserve a recovery reference, preview the smallest change, then verify both tree behavior and history topology.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Local series has commits parse → test → docs atop old main; new main changes the parser contract. Plan replay, conflict tests, range-diff, recovery ref, and force-with-lease boundary. A defensible solution applies Interactive Rebase, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a history-rewrite plan and verification graph.

content.code:
git branch recovery/pre-rebase
git rebase --rebase-merges main
git range-diff recovery/pre-rebase...HEAD
git diff recovery/pre-rebase^{tree} HEAD^{tree}
content.caption: Runnable repository-state trace example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Interactive rebase can safely reorder dependent commits without changing behavior. This fails the Interactive Rebase contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

steps.title: Build Interactive Rebase evidence
steps.instruction: Append a safe repository investigation headed "# Evidence: git-interactive-rebase-1upb96" with a concrete hypothesis, one bounded simulator command, and an observation that changes the next decision.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Interactive Rebase into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: git-interactive-rebase-1upb96
# hypothesis: [replace with one falsifiable cause]
# command: choose one bounded simulator observation
# observation: [replace with the result that changes the next decision]

steps.title: Defend the Interactive Rebase model
steps.instruction: Write the theory evidence note for Interactive Rebase: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Local series has commits parse → test → docs atop old main; new main changes the parser contract. Plan replay, conflict tests, range-diff, recovery ref, and force-with-lease boundary.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Use pick, reword, edit, squash, fixup, drop, exec, and reorder with dependency and test awareness.
